quick_facts.label: Programme level
quick_facts.value: M.Tech is a postgraduate engineering programme. At IIT Bombay, the M.Tech structure is described as providing advanced theoretical, practical and research-oriented knowledge and includes coursework plus a thesis/project component.

quick_facts.label: Common Mechanical Engineering specialisations
quick_facts.value: Specialisation names vary by institution. IIT Bombay’s Mechanical Engineering department lists Thermal & Fluids Engineering, Design Engineering, and Manufacturing Engineering.

quick_facts.label: Indicative duration
quick_facts.value: A regular Teaching Assistantship M.Tech in Mechanical Engineering is two years at IIT Bombay; selected research-assistantship and sponsored categories there are two or three years. Duration is institution- and admission-category-specific.

quick_facts.label: Primary admission route
quick_facts.value: GATE is the entrance test used for IIT Bombay M.Tech admissions. Mechanical Engineering admission may also involve department-level shortlisting, written tests, and/or interviews depending on the category and institution.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: Eligibility is set by each institute and specialisation rather than by one universal national rule. For IIT Bombay’s 2026–27 Mechanical Engineering M.Tech admissions, the qualifying degree requirements differ by specialisation: Thermal & Fluids Engineering accepts specified Aeronautical/Aerospace, Automobile, Chemical, or Mechanical Engineering backgrounds; Design Engineering accepts specified Aeronautical/Aerospace, Automobile, Mechanical, Machine Tool, or Production/Industrial Engineering backgrounds; and Manufacturing Engineering accepts specified Mechanical, Machine Tool, or Production/Industrial Engineering backgrounds.

For its regular M.Tech route, IIT Bombay identifies GATE as the entrance test. Its Mechanical Engineering department also states that sponsored applicants may qualify with a valid GATE score or two years of relevant experience after the qualifying B.E./B.Tech degree, subject to that category’s requirements. Applicants should therefore verify the current brochure for the target department, specialisation, category, accepted GATE paper, marks threshold, and work-experience rules before applying.
bullets: A relevant bachelor’s engineering qualification is commonly required, but accepted branches can differ across Thermal/Fluids, Design, and Manufacturing tracks.
A valid GATE score is central to regular IIT Bombay M.Tech admission; sponsored and other special categories can follow different rules.

Self-financed routes may have different GATE requirements: NIT Warangal’s 2026 self-financed M.Tech process lists Mechanical Engineering and requires a department-based written test/interview process, with a GATE score card requested where available.

sections.heading: Duration and study format
paragraphs: The standard full-time format is commonly two years, illustrated by IIT Bombay’s Mechanical Engineering Teaching Assistantship and IIT-B.Tech. admission categories. IIT Bombay also lists three-year Research Assistantship and Research Assistantship-through-Project options, while sponsored candidates may have a two- or three-year duration. These are examples of category-dependent programme design, not a single duration rule for every Indian institution.
bullets: Regular full-time/Teaching Assistantship example: two years at IIT Bombay.
Research Assistantship examples: three years at IIT Bombay.
Sponsored-category example: two or three years at IIT Bombay.

sections.heading: Curriculum and specialisation
paragraphs: Mechanical Engineering M.Tech curricula are specialised and research/project oriented. IIT Bombay describes advanced compulsory courses, electives, and an individual M.Tech project lasting fourteen months in the final phase; it identifies the project as typically research-and-development oriented. Its department offers Thermal & Fluids Engineering, Design Engineering, and Manufacturing Engineering specialisations.

For example, IIT Bombay’s published Thermal & Fluids Engineering structure includes Fluid Dynamics, Advanced Thermodynamics & Combustion, Advanced Heat Transfer, Computational Methods in Thermal & Fluids Engineering, Mathematical Methods in Engineering, laboratory work, electives, and the M.Tech project. Course titles, credits, laboratories, and thesis requirements should be checked in the current curriculum of the admitting institute.
bullets: Thermal and fluids study can include fluid mechanics, thermodynamics, combustion, heat transfer, and computation.
Design and manufacturing are separate named specialisations at IIT Bombay.
An individual research/development project is a major component in the IIT Bombay example.

sections.heading: Admission process
paragraphs: For the 2026–27 cycle, IIT Bombay states that GATE is its M.Tech entrance test and that applicants for Teaching Assistantship and Research Assistantship categories must register on the Common Offer Acceptance Portal (COAP); offers for those categories are made through COAP. Its Mechanical Engineering department states that M.Tech students are admitted once a year in July.

Selection mechanics depend on the category. IIT Bombay Mechanical Engineering lists Teaching Assistantship selection as 70% GATE score and 30% interview performance, while sponsored candidates are selected through an interview. NIT Warangal’s 2026 self-financed Mechanical Engineering process separately scheduled offline written tests and interviews. Application windows, cut-offs, seat counts, interview rules, and fees change by institute and admission year, so applicants should rely on the current official notice rather than past cut-off or deadline information.
bullets: Identify the target specialisation and its accepted qualifying/GATE disciplines.
Check the institute’s current M.Tech brochure and departmental notice for category-specific eligibility and selection.
Where applicable, register on COAP and complete the institute application separately.
Keep qualifying-degree records, GATE score card where required, category certificates, and experience/sponsorship documents ready for verification.
